repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1: Spearman-band Pr(Y1>Y0) transport-envelope proposal; close concordance-band ancestor but different event, different association measure, and no single-point Kendall C(u1,u0) GPN reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 gives continuous-outcome GPN sharp bounds and copula sensitivity; NelsenQuesadaMolinaRodriguezLallenaUbedaFlores2001/NelsenUbedaFlores2004 give pointwise Kendall copula bounds; none states the causal population counterfactual Kendall-band sharp set.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 has no Gaussian-subfamily comparison for the population GPN tau-band endpoint.

published_prior_art.cite: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 and HeLi2026 motivate parametric copula sensitivity; the subset claim is a routine comparator formalization once Theorem 1 is available.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 has Frechet/Makarov-adjacent sanity material for a different strict-benefit functional, not this population GPN denominator-weighted target.

published_prior_art.cite: Frechet-Hoeffding/Nelsen2006 already give the copula reduction; the causal ratio specialization is a sanity check rather than independent novelty.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 contains outer-envelope/bootstrap fragments for Spearman transport endpoints, not EIFs for Kendall point-evaluation GPN ratio endpoints.

published_prior_art.cite: HeLi2026 derives EIFs and one-step estimators for fixed parametric-copula ordinal functionals; DornGuoKallus2022 gives sharp-bound orthogonal sensitivity estimation for ATE bounds, not Kendall-band cross-world GPN endpoints.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 explicitly left nonsmooth concordance-curve endpoint inference as a promise gap and for a different Spearman transport target.

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 supplies the general intersection-bound inference template; HeLi2026 gives pointwise fixed-copula bands, not uniform Kendall-envelope inference with active-piece switches.

soundness_flags.label: ass:nuisance-rate-m1e / ass:nuisance-rate-m0e
soundness_flags.one_line: The exact active-Nelsen-piece second-order remainder condition is tagged standard with HeLi2026, but HeLi2026 covers fixed parametric copulas and does not support this family-free Kendall-envelope condition as a standard named assumption.

soundness_flags.label: thm:regular-endpoint-eif
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem bundles the population EIF claim with estimator nuisance-rate assumptions; rates are needed for one-step asymptotic normality, not for the canonical-gradient identification itself.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:conditional-tau-band
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The constant conditional Kendall-tau band is an elicited sensitivity restriction, not something derived from the observed law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The sharp-set claim is accepted only under the stated conditional Kendall-band sensitivity model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-piece-lower / ass:regular-piece-upper
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Scalar root-n endpoint inference is conditioned on remaining inside smooth active copula-bound pieces away from kink sets.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The regular EIF and one-step CLT are accepted only as regular-regime inference claims, with switch-point behavior delegated to the uniform-tau conjecture.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:uniform-tau-curve-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Uniform inference through finitely many active-piece switches is an open solve target, not a proved consequence of the scalar EIF theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The tau-curve confidence-band claim is bound to proving a directional derivative and bootstrap validity for the switched endpoint map.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Retag the two nuisance-rate assumptions as novel/tailored active-envelope conditions or replace the cite with a DML condition plus a separate lemma deriving the nonlinear Nelsen-map remainder.
Split Theorem 2 into a population EIF statement and a one-step CLT statement, so the EIF does not appear to depend on estimator-rate assumptions.
Add a small worked Kendall-bound endpoint exhibit at fixed (u1,u0,tau) showing how the Nelsen formula maps into theta_L/theta_U for a two-stratum or no-X example.
For the uniform-tau conjecture, name the candidate directional derivative or active-piece max/min functional that the multiplier bootstrap is meant to approximate.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the novelty clears the field floor, but the estimation assumptions need retagging/splitting before Stage 0.
